title: CI/CD Release Pipeline — GitHub Actions cross-platform binary builds
description: Create GitHub Actions workflow to build and release hex-cli + hex-nexus binaries for macOS ARM64 and Linux x86_64. Triggered on version tags. Uploads artifacts to GitHub Releases. This is the production distribution mechanism.

phases.name: GitHub Actions workflow
phases.description: Create .github/workflows/release.yml that builds hex-cli and hex-nexus for macOS ARM64 (macos-14) and Linux x86_64 (ubuntu-22.04) on push to version tags (v*.*.*). Uses cross-compilation via cargo with appropriate targets.

tasks.name: Create release.yml GitHub Actions workflow
tasks.description:
Create .github/workflows/release.yml with the following workflow:

1. Trigger: `on: push: tags: ['v*.*.*']` and `workflow_dispatch` (for manual testing)

2. Jobs matrix:
   - `build-macos-arm64`: runs-on: macos-14, target: aarch64-apple-darwin
   - `build-linux-x86_64`: runs-on: ubuntu-22.04, target: x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu

3. Each job:
   - actions/checkout@v4
   - actions/cache for ~/.cargo/registry + target/
   - rustup target add <target>
   - cargo build --release --target <target> -p hex-cli
   - cargo build --release --target <target> -p hex-nexus
   - tar czf hex-<version>-<target>.tar.gz -C target/<target>/release hex hex-nexus
   - actions/upload-artifact@v4

4. Job `release` (needs: [build-macos-arm64, build-linux-x86_64]):
   - actions/download-artifact@v4 for all artifacts
   - softprops/action-gh-release@v2 to create GitHub release with all tarballs
   - Generate SHA256 checksums

5. For Linux: install build dependencies (pkg-config, libssl-dev, libsqlite3-dev)
6. For macOS: MACOSX_DEPLOYMENT_TARGET=11.0

Note: The existing .github/workflows/release.yml may already exist — read it first and extend/replace as needed.
tasks.done_condition: release.yml exists; workflow triggers on version tags; builds both platforms; uploads to GitHub Releases

phases.name: Version management
phases.description: Add a version bump script and ensure Cargo.toml versions are consistent across all crates. Create a release checklist.

tasks.name: Create scripts/release.sh version bump script
tasks.description:
Create scripts/release.sh that:
1. Accepts a version argument: `./scripts/release.sh 0.5.0`
2. Updates version in all Cargo.toml files (hex-cli, hex-nexus, hex-agent, hex-core, hex-parser, hex-desktop)
3. Runs `cargo check` to verify Cargo.lock updates
4. Creates a git commit: `chore(release): bump version to v{version}`
5. Creates a git tag: `v{version}`
6. Prints next steps: `git push origin main --tags` to trigger the release workflow

Make it executable (chmod +x).
tasks.done_condition: scripts/release.sh exists and is executable; updates all Cargo.toml versions consistently

tasks.name: Verify crate versions are consistent
tasks.description: Check all workspace member Cargo.toml files (hex-cli, hex-nexus, hex-agent, hex-core, hex-parser). They should all have matching versions. If they don't, update them to be consistent (use the current hex-cli version as the canonical version). Also verify the workspace Cargo.toml has a [workspace.package] version field that all members inherit if using workspace inheritance.
tasks.done_condition: All crate versions consistent; cargo check passes

phases.name: Installation script
phases.description: Create a one-line install script that downloads the appropriate binary for the user's platform and installs it to /usr/local/bin.

tasks.name: Create scripts/install.sh
tasks.description:
Create scripts/install.sh:

```bash
#!/bin/bash
set -e
VERSION=${1:-latest}
OS=$(uname -s | tr '[:upper:]' '[:lower:]')
ARCH=$(uname -m)

# Resolve target
if [ "$OS" = "darwin" ] && [ "$ARCH" = "arm64" ]; then
  TARGET="aarch64-apple-darwin"
elif [ "$OS" = "linux" ] && [ "$ARCH" = "x86_64" ]; then
  TARGET="x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu"
else
  echo "Unsupported platform: $OS/$ARCH"
  exit 1
fi

# Get latest version if not specified
if [ "$VERSION" = "latest" ]; then
  VERSION=$(curl -s https://api.github.com/repos/OWNER/hex/releases/latest | grep tag_name | cut -d'"' -f4)
fi

URL="https://github.com/OWNER/hex/releases/download/${VERSION}/hex-${VERSION}-${TARGET}.tar.gz"
echo "Downloading hex ${VERSION} for ${TARGET}..."
curl -L "$URL" | tar xz -C /tmp
sudo mv /tmp/hex /usr/local/bin/hex
sudo mv /tmp/hex-nexus /usr/local/bin/hex-nexus
echo "hex ${VERSION} installed successfully"
hex --version
```

Replace OWNER with the actual GitHub organization/user. Make executable.
tasks.done_condition: scripts/install.sh exists; is executable; detects platform correctly; downloads from GitHub Releases

successCriteria: git push origin v0.5.0 triggers workflow and produces downloadable binaries
scripts/install.sh downloads and installs hex in one command
